sglang.title: Support multi-node DP attention

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
# node 1
python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite-Instruct --trust-remote-code --tp 4 --enable-dp-attention --nnodes 2 --node-rank 0 --dist-init-addr sgl-m1:20000

# node 2
python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite-Instruct --trust-r

sglang.author: CatherineSue
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

TokenizerManager should check for input_ids is not None instead of obj.input_ids as obj.input_ids could be None.
When max_tokens + input_len exceeds the model's context length, there should be a ValueError.
Silently truncating for inputs that exceed the maximum length in scheduler.py wil

sglang.title: Enable CPU device on SGLang
sglang.author: chunyuan-w
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

This PR enables CPU device on SGLang.
Currently we fallback attention and MoE to the torch native backend and make the functionality work on CPU.
We will submit follow-up PRs to provide optimized kernels to further improvement the performance.
For vllm installation for CPU, users could f

vllm.title: Various cosmetic/comment fixes
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: These are some cosmetic issues leftover from recently merged PRs

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Remove hardcoded `head_size=256` for Deepseek v2 and v3
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #12015 (link existing issues this PR will resolve)

FA2 backend has supported head_size=192, so there is no need to pad qk to head_dim=256 anymore.
Remove head_size=256 limitation and qk padding for Deepseek-V2/V3 attention.

vllm.title: Support torchrun and SPMD-style offline inference
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Per the request from RLHF community #11400 , there's a strong need to co-locate vllm process and another process like training processes.
This PR enables the support, by allowing vllm launched in a torchrun-compatible way .

vllm.title: [core] LLM.collective_rpc interface and RLHF example
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR introduces a user-facing API LLM.collective_rpc so that users can easily extend the functionality of vLLM.
The workflow is:

Users inherit vLLM's worker to add new functions. These functions have access to internal states of the workers.
Users call LLM.collective_rpc on their newly defined f

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix max image feature size for Llava-one-vision
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt: It was discovered that some images may generate embeddings with size bigger than the assumed max feature size, and therefore crash the server since these requests will never get scheduled because of #11895. This PR adds a temporary placeholder until the processor fixes this issue completely.

vllm.title: [misc] Add LoRA kernel micro benchmarks
vllm.author: varun-sundar-rabindranath
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Add LoRA kernel micro benchmarks for tuning/optimizing LoRA kernels

The benchmarking script creates a pool of tensors for each kernel argument and uses the tensors in-order for benchmarking. Having a bigger argument pool helps in mitigating the caching effects during benchmarking.
The benchmarking

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix a path bug in disaggregated prefill example script.
vllm.author: KuntaiDu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
After #11923 , the path of disaggregated prefill example script moved into online_serving folder. Update the example script to make sure it is functional.
Also add set -xe to make sure it crashes instead of failing silently when error occurred in the script.

vllm.title: [V1] Move more control of kv cache initialization from model_executor to EngineCore
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This pr changes the workflow of EngineCore._initialize_kv_caches to enable more flexible control of kv cache format in the future.
It is splitted from #11938 and is a preparation for #11382
Original workflow:
num_gpu_blocks, _ = self.model_executor.determine_num_available_blocks()
self.model_executo
